description: Ambiguous natural-language queries for testing QueryPlanner slot extraction, retrieval strategy flags, and evidence requirements.

matcher_semantics.equals: Actual value must exactly equal expected value.
matcher_semantics.equals_unordered: Actual list must contain exactly the same items, order-insensitive.
matcher_semantics.contains_all: Actual list must include every listed item; extra items are allowed.
matcher_semantics.contains_any: Actual list must include at least one listed item.
matcher_semantics.not_contains_any: Actual list must include none of the listed items.
matcher_semantics.gte: Actual numeric value must be greater than or equal to this value.
matcher_semantics.lte: Actual numeric value must be less than or equal to this value.

cases.question: Did Apple sell more than last year?
What changed compared with last year?

note.equals: This is intentionally ambiguous; revenue may also be acceptable if your product treats 'money' as sales.

cases.question: What are Apple's latest risk factors?

note.equals: Current rule planner may miss this unless risk-like aliases are expanded.
This is intentionally fuzzy; risk or prose may be acceptable depending on product policy.
This may fail until liquidity intent is implemented.
This may fail until liquidity/cash-position support is implemented.

cases.question: Which part of Apple did better?
苹果利润率为什么变化？
